title: Two Sum
description: Which data structure gives an efficient solution?

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Which data structure is commonly used to quickly detect duplicates?

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: What strategy gives the optimal profit in one pass?
hint: Track the lowest price seen so far.

title: Find the Bug
description:
What is wrong with this code?

numbers = [1,2,3]
print(numbers[3])
hint: Remember that list indexes start at 0.

title: Find the Largest Number
description:
Given an array of integers, return the largest number in the array.

Example:
Input: [3,9,2,15,7]
Output: 15
hint: Create a variable to store the largest value and compare each number while looping.

title: Move Zeroes
description:
Given an integer array, move all 0's to the end of it while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements.

Example:
Input: [0,1,0,3,12]
Output: [1,3,12,0,0]
hint: Use two pointers. One pointer tracks the position where the next non-zero element should be placed.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description:
Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i].

You must solve it without using division and in O(n) time.

Example:
Input: [1,2,3,4]
Output: [24,12,8,6]
hint: Use prefix and suffix products to avoid division.

title: 3Sum
description:
Given an integer array nums, return all unique triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] where the three numbers add up to zero.

Example:
Input: [-1,0,1,2,-1,-4]
Output: [[-1,-1,2],[-1,0,1]]
hint: Sort the array first and use the two pointer technique.

title: Trapping Rain Water
description:
Given an elevation map where each bar represents height, calculate how much water can be trapped after raining.

Example:
Input: [0,1,0,2,1,0,1,3,2,1,2,1]
Output: 6
hint: Use two pointers or prefix maximum arrays to track water boundaries.

title: Maximum Subarray Sum with Constraints
description: Find the maximum sum of a contiguous subarray when additional constraints limit valid selections.
hint: Consider using Kadane's Algorithm combined with additional tracking.
